Off Calendar Jun-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS PRADEEP GANDHI AND MERIDIAN FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO VACATE VOID JUDGMENT is OFF CALENDAR, moot. Stipulation to Vacate Judgment on file. Parties to present a hard copy of the order on the stipulation to the Court for execution forthwith.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-27-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Judgment (Default)
Off Calendar Mar-27-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendant Pradeep Gandhi and Defendant Meridian Financial Services, Inc.

Dept.505: Judge: Cecilia P. Castellanos; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" commencing at 9:08:14 a.m., pausing at 9:25:52 a.m., resuming at 9:31:24 a.m. and concluding at 9:50:28 a.m. (D505)

Joseph P. Corey, representing Plaintiff Mary Mettler, who was present, placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Pradeep Gandhi not appearing.
Defendant Meridian Financial Services, Inc. not appearing.

Plaintiff's complaint for Trespass filed November 5, 2020.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendant Pradeep Gandhi on November 06, 2020; effectuated on November 16, 2020.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendant Meridian Financial Services, Inc. on September 10, 2020; effectuated on September 21, 2020.
Dismissal with prejudice as to Defendant Arcon Construction corporation filed September 27, 2021.
Default entered as to Defendant Pradeep Gandhi on April 15, 2021.
Default entered as to Defendant Meridian Financial Services, Inc. on March 14, 2022.
Doe Defendants dismissed January 21,2023.

The Court reviews the filings submitted and filed.

Court inquires as to the attorney's fees.

Counsel offers proof as to costs prayed for.

Court reviews the amount prayed for and compares to the proposed judgment.

**Court goes off the record**

Discussion of the statement of damages between Court and Counsel.

Plaintiff submits the following exhibit/s for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Plaintiff's declaration.
Exhibit 2: Plaintiff's receipts and photographs.

The Clerk administers the witness oath to Plaintiff Mary Mettler.

Counsel examines plaintiff.

Plaintiff testifies as to emotional distress.

After hearing the testimony, reviewing the declaration and considering the evidence, the Court finds that Plaintiff has met the burden of proof.

The Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against Defendants as follows:
Damages $17,207.00
Interest $0
Attorney's Fees $0
Costs $1,573.00
General Damages/Emotional Distress $15,000.00

Total Judgment $33,780.00

Proposed judgment signed.

Parties stipulate to the return of the exhibits.
